findings.title: Spoofed local processes can choose the file dscli tails
findings.impact: `find_worker_pid` accepts the first matching process whose command line contains the requested `-worker_address=` and is not `dscli`, without verifying that it is the datasystem worker binary or owned by an expected user. A local user can run a dummy process with that argument plus attacker-chosen `--log_dir`/`--log_filename`; when a more privileged operator runs `dscli logs -w` for that address, `resolve_log_path` reads those spoofed flags and `tail` prints the selected `.INFO.log` path with the operator's permissions. That turns this read-only helper into a local confused-deputy file disclosure path.
findings.fix: Only accept PIDs that are verified to be the real worker, for example by checking `/proc/<pid>/exe` or argv[0] against the installed `datasystem_worker` path and rejecting mismatched owners/paths. Also validate the resolved log path against the worker log directory policy before invoking `tail`, and fail closed if the process identity or path cannot be confirmed.
